description: Replace the resource-name-only document-reference failure surface in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs` with a path-aware per-occurrence contract for DMS-982. Acceptance: the new contract can represent every failing document-reference instance with its concrete JSON path, target resource, and an internal reason enum at minimum covering `Missing` and `IncompatibleTargetType`; the contract is usable by both POST and PUT handlers; and descriptor failure contracts remain available for existing 400-style mapping.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit tests that construct the new result types and confirm every failing occurrence can be represented without collapsing to unique resource names.

description: Update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs`, and any shared response helpers so path-aware document-reference failures become 409 responses keyed by concrete JSON path instances. Acceptance: existing status semantics stay unchanged, every failing path instance is emitted in `validationErrors`, duplicate referential ids at different JSON locations produce multiple entries, and descriptor failures continue to map to 400 validation responses.
steps-to-verify: Add handler tests covering POST and PUT document-reference failures with multiple concrete paths for the same missing referential id.

description: Extend the backend-to-Core descriptor failure contract and Core handler mapping so DMS-982 can distinguish `Missing` versus `DescriptorTypeMismatch` per failing descriptor path while preserving the current client-visible 400 status and overall validation-error shape. Acceptance: `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs` carry enough per-path reason data for Core to render different messages; `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs` emit a different validation message for type mismatch than for missing descriptors; and no other client-exposed contract differences are introduced.
steps-to-verify: Add handler/result tests covering both missing-descriptor and descriptor-type-mismatch paths for POST and PUT, asserting the same 400/error shape with different messages.

description: Add the reusable relational write-prerequisite resolver service and contracts for DMS-982 without wiring a new relational `IDocumentStoreRepository` path. Acceptance: the service accepts request-scoped extracted references plus `MappingSet`/resource context, performs per-request memoization and dedupe, preserves per-occurrence diagnostics in a `ResolvedReferenceSet`, and exposes a narrow adapter seam so future auth/write prerequisite batching can append SQL without redesigning the service contract.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for request-scoped dedupe and memoization behavior using fake adapter results.

description: Define the shared request-scoped relational access contract used by the DMS-982 resolver and dialect adapters. Acceptance: shared resolver/adapter code depends on a narrow abstraction for command execution and result reading rather than repository operations, the contract is small enough to support future batched write-prerequisite composition without redesign, and contract-focused unit tests validate fake/in-memory implementations used by resolver tests.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering the shared relational access abstraction and fake/test implementations used by the resolver.

description: Implement the PostgreSQL request-scoped relational access path behind the shared DMS-982 contract. Acceptance: PostgreSQL resolver commands execute against the current request's data source/connection path, the implementation reuses existing request-scoped PostgreSQL infrastructure instead of introducing repository wiring, and focused unit tests cover connection selection and command execution behavior needed by the resolver.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for PostgreSQL relational access behavior through the shared contract.

description: Implement the SQL Server request-scoped relational access path behind the shared DMS-982 contract. Acceptance: SQL Server resolver commands execute from the active request-scoped connection-string path without introducing full repository wiring, the implementation exposes the same shared contract used by PostgreSQL, and focused unit tests cover connection creation and command execution behavior needed by the resolver.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for SQL Server relational access behavior through the shared contract.

description: Build the request-scoped resolved reference surface that downstream write execution will consume. Acceptance: given extracted reference occurrences plus deduped resolver results, runtime can materialize a deterministic successful-resolution view keyed by extracted reference occurrence/path; duplicate referential ids at different JSON locations remain separate occurrences in the surface; and unresolved or incompatible occurrences are excluded from successful resolutions while still remaining available in failure diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering repeated referential ids across multiple concrete JSON paths and ensuring only successful resolutions populate the successful-resolution surface.

description: Implement metadata helpers that derive allowed target `ResourceKeyId` sets for document-reference validation from the runtime mapping set and derived relational model. Acceptance: concrete targets resolve to exactly one allowed `ResourceKeyId`, abstract/superclass targets resolve to the allowed concrete member keys for the hierarchy, descriptor resources are excluded from document-reference compatibility checks, and missing mapping-set metadata fails fast with deterministic errors.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering concrete targets, abstract targets, and missing-resource failures using compiled mapping fixtures.

description: Implement the PostgreSQL bulk lookup SQL shape and parameterization for DMS-982. Acceptance: document and descriptor referential ids can be emitted into a set-based PostgreSQL lookup against `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, no per-reference query loop is introduced, the SQL shape can later participate in a larger batched write-prerequisite command, and focused unit tests cover empty, small, and large deduped lookup sets plus deterministic parameter ordering.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for PostgreSQL SQL shape generation and parameter binding across representative lookup sizes.

description: Implement PostgreSQL adapter execution and raw row projection over the shared resolver contract. Acceptance: the adapter executes the PostgreSQL lookup through the shared relational access seam, projects rows from `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, `dms.Document`, and `dms.Descriptor` into shared raw result records, returns enough membership/type data for later shared failure classification, and preserves deduped lookup semantics while retaining per-occurrence inputs for diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for PostgreSQL raw result projection covering found, missing, alias-row, and descriptor-membership row shapes.

description: Implement the SQL Server small-list lookup strategy for DMS-982 using a simple parameterized approach beneath safe thresholds. Acceptance: small deduped lookup sets execute without risking the 2,100-parameter limit, document and descriptor lookups share one resolver-oriented raw row contract, no per-reference query loop is introduced, and focused unit tests cover deterministic parameterization and row projection for representative small inputs.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for the SQL Server small-list strategy and raw row projection contract.

description: Implement the SQL Server threshold-safe bulk lookup strategy for large DMS-982 reference sets. Acceptance: large deduped lookup sets switch to a bulk-safe strategy before risking the 2,100-parameter limit, the strategy returns the same raw adapter row contract as the small-list path, and focused unit tests cover threshold crossing and deterministic batching behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests that exercise the SQL Server threshold-crossing path and bulk-safe lookup mechanics.

description: Implement SQL Server adapter orchestration that selects between the small-list and bulk strategies and exposes the shared raw resolver result contract. Acceptance: strategy selection is deterministic, the adapter returns the membership/type data required for later shared failure classification, and focused unit tests assert raw-result parity with PostgreSQL across found, missing, incompatible-target, and descriptor-membership row shapes.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for SQL Server strategy selection and raw-result parity with PostgreSQL.

description: Implement shared failure classification over adapter results for both document and descriptor references. Acceptance: unresolved document references and wrong-type document matches surface through the new path-aware document-reference contract with internal reasons kept distinct for tests/logging, descriptor failures distinguish at least `Missing` versus `DescriptorTypeMismatch`, and every failing concrete JSON path instance is preserved even when the underlying lookup key was deduped.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that exercise mixed document/descriptor failures and repeated failing paths sharing the same deduped key.

description: Define the shared resolver composition surface without yet wiring repository request paths. Acceptance: the resolver, relational access seam, and dialect adapter factory/contracts are registered behind a narrow interface that both production PostgreSQL wiring and SQL Server test wiring can consume; the surface does not require `IDocumentStoreRepository` integration; and unit tests can build a service collection around the new shared contract without pulling in full frontend startup.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that build a minimal service collection around the shared resolver composition surface and adapter factory.

description: Wire the shared DMS-982 composition surface into the PostgreSQL production registration path. Acceptance: when PostgreSQL datastore services are registered with `UseRelationalBackend` enabled, the prerequisite resolver, relational access seam, and PostgreSQL adapter factory can all be resolved from the application service collection without introducing a new relational repository execution path.
steps-to-verify: Add or update DI tests covering PostgreSQL service registration through the current frontend/bootstrap path.

description: Provide SQL Server registration/test wiring for the shared DMS-982 composition surface. Acceptance: SQL Server-focused tests can construct the resolver, relational access seam, and SQL Server adapter factory through the same shared contract/factory seam used by PostgreSQL, without requiring new frontend datastore bootstrap or `IDocumentStoreRepository` request-path integration in this story.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that resolve the SQL Server adapter factory and resolver from a service collection using the shared composition surface.

description: Add shared resolver fixture models and seed helpers for DMS-982 that both dialect-specific integration harnesses can consume. Acceptance: shared helpers describe compact resolver datasets for `dms.ResourceKey`, `dms.Document`, `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, `dms.Descriptor`, and any required abstract alias/member rows; the helpers do not own dialect-specific schema provisioning or reset; and they can express both successful and fail-closed compatibility scenarios needed by later integration tasks.
steps-to-verify: Add shared fixture/seeding helpers reused by both PostgreSQL and SQL Server resolver integration tests.

description: Add PostgreSQL-specific provisioning and reset support for the focused DMS-982 integration harness. Acceptance: the PostgreSQL integration project can create the minimal resolver schema objects required by the shared fixtures, seed repeatable test datasets, and reset state between tests without relying on the full DMS E2E stack.
steps-to-verify: Add PostgreSQL integration fixture setup that provisions the minimal resolver test database shape used by the shared fixtures.

description: Add SQL Server-specific provisioning and reset support for the focused DMS-982 integration harness. Acceptance: the SQL Server integration project can create the minimal resolver schema objects required by the shared fixtures, seed repeatable test datasets, and reset state between tests without relying on the full DMS E2E stack.
steps-to-verify: Add SQL Server integration fixture setup that provisions the minimal resolver test database shape used by the shared fixtures.

description: Add focused PostgreSQL integration coverage for document-reference resolution behavior. Acceptance: tests cover request-local dedupe versus per-path diagnostics, missing document references at multiple concrete JSON locations, concrete-target mismatch, and abstract/superclass alias resolution with fail-closed compatibility validation.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the PostgreSQL integration test connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration`.

description: Add focused PostgreSQL integration coverage for descriptor validation behavior. Acceptance: tests cover descriptor membership failure, descriptor discriminator/type mismatch, and parity of public failure mapping versus the shared classifier contract.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the PostgreSQL integration test connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration`.

description: Add focused PostgreSQL integration coverage for bulk lookup behavior. Acceptance: tests cover at least one large deduped lookup set that still resolves through the bulk PostgreSQL lookup path and confirm repeated per-path diagnostics remain intact after dedupe.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the PostgreSQL integration test connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration`.

description: Add focused SQL Server integration coverage for document-reference resolution behavior. Acceptance: tests cover request-local dedupe versus per-path diagnostics, missing document references at multiple concrete JSON locations, concrete-target mismatch, and abstract/superclass alias resolution with fail-closed compatibility validation at the same public-result level as PostgreSQL.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the SQL Server admin connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration`.

description: Add focused SQL Server integration coverage for descriptor validation behavior. Acceptance: tests cover descriptor membership failure, descriptor discriminator/type mismatch, and parity of public failure mapping versus PostgreSQL where the adapter strategy differs under the hood.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the SQL Server admin connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration`.

description: Add focused SQL Server integration coverage for threshold-sensitive bulk lookup behavior. Acceptance: tests cover at least one threshold-crossing large deduped lookup set that exercises the SQL Server bulk-safe strategy and confirm repeated per-path diagnostics remain intact after dedupe.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the SQL Server admin connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration`.
